source_test: HEC.FDA.Model/metrics/AlternativeResults.cs (no upstream unit test file exists for this class; oracle-gate-reproduced against the real AlternativeResults/ScenarioResults/StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile directly)
note: Phase 6 Task 6. Constructs an AlternativeResults directly via the internal 'public for testing' (StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile, id, analysisYears, periodOfAnalysis, isNull) ctor, then assigns base_year_scenario_results_/future_year_scenario_results_ (both caller-set fields per the class comment) and scenarios_are_identical_ directly -- this exercises the delegation logic in isolation, independent of where the flag would actually be computed (Alternative::annualization_compute, Task 9, not yet ported). `base_year_impact_areas`/`future_year_impact_areas` are each a list of entries in EXACTLY the impact_area_scenario_results.json construct/consequence_realizations/aep_observations/stage_observations shape (reused via make_impact_area_scenario_results_case, same as scenario_results.json's own `impact_areas` field) -- one ImpactAreaScenarioResults per entry, add_results'd into a fresh ScenarioResults in order. `eqad_results` is a study_area_consequences_by_quantile.json-shaped {consequence_results: [...]} object -- one AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile per entry (construction order) via the (string,string,Empirical,int,ConsequenceType,RiskType) ctor, with Empirical.SampleMean force-set to the entry's own `sample_mean` field, collected into a StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile via its (List<AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile>) ctor -- this becomes the AlternativeResults ctor's `eqad_results` argument directly (`is_null=false`). Both cases below reuse IDENTICAL base_year/future_year/eqad_results construction data -- only `scenarios_are_identical` differs -- so any difference between the two cases' `sample_mean_eqad`/`get_eqad_distribution_sample_mean` results is caused SOLELY by the delegation branch flipping, not by different underlying data. `method` dispatches (args always [damage_category_or_null, asset_category_or_null, impact_area_id], ConsequenceType.Damage/RiskType.Total implicit at every level, matching how both the ScenarioResults consequence realizations and the eqad_results entries were built): `sample_mean_eqad` -- THE delegator: scenarios_are_identical_ ? base_year_scenario_results_.SampleMeanExpectedAnnualConsequences(...) : eqad_results_.SampleMeanDamage(...); `sample_mean_base_year_ead`/`sample_mean_future_year_ead` -- straight pass-throughs to base_year_scenario_results_/future_year_scenario_results_.SampleMeanExpectedAnnualConsequences(...), NEVER branching on scenarios_are_identical_ (proving base and future stay independently readable regardless of the flag); `get_eqad_distribution_sample_mean` -- the Empirical-returning sibling of sample_mean_eqad (scenarios_are_identical_ ? base_year_scenario_results_.GetConsequencesDistribution(...).SampleMean : eqad_results_.GetAggregateEmpiricalDistribution(...).SampleMean). eqad_results_'s two entries' SampleMean fields (250.0 covering impact area 7, 100.0 covering impact area 8) are force-set to values that don't coincide with either scenario's own expected-annual-consequences figure, and StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile.SampleMeanDamage/GetAggregateEmpiricalDistribution().SampleMean over the wildcard filter both reduce to a left-to-right SUM of the matched entries' own SampleMean fields (250.0 + 100.0 = 350.0 exactly -- see study_area_consequences_by_quantile.json's own note for why StackEmpiricalDistributions' SampleMean bookkeeping is itself just that sum), so `get_eqad_distribution_sample_mean`'s eqad-branch value (350.0) is a safe hand-derived cross-check even though every other assertion is gate-PIN-captured (the ScenarioResults SampleMeanExpectedAnnualConsequences figures route through real consequence-histogram binning, not a simple average of the raw realizations, so those are never hand-derived). All values PIN unless noted, gate-captured, never hand-derived beyond the one documented exception above.
